info.name: Stock Price Analyzer
info.description:
Exercises every documented contract surface of the Stock Price Analyzer API.

**Base URL.** Defaults to the deployed Railway URL (`https://stock-analyzer-production-b678.up.railway.app`). Override the `baseUrl` collection variable to point at `http://localhost:3000` if running the app locally via `npm run start:dev`.

**Folders.**
- **Health** — liveness check, no rate limiting.
- **Metadata** — dataset coverage and intervalSeconds.
- **Happy path** — full window plus three engineered sub-windows (trending AM, lunch lull, sell-off) demonstrating the algorithm on different price phases.
- **Errors** — one request per documented error code (`INVALID_RANGE`, `OUT_OF_BOUNDS`, `INVALID_TIMESTAMP`).

All requests use second-precision UTC timestamps with the trailing `Z` suffix (e.g. `2026-04-22T09:30:00Z`). Sub-second precision is rejected with `INVALID_TIMESTAMP`.

variable.description: Base URL of the Stock Price Analyzer. Defaults to the deployed Railway URL. Override to `http://localhost:3000` if running the app locally via `npm run start:dev`.

request.description: Liveness check. Expected 200 with `{"status":"ok"}`. Outside the `/api` prefix and skipped by the throttler.
Dataset metadata. Expected 200 with `{ symbol, name, currency, from, to, intervalSeconds }`. Throttled at 120 requests/minute per IP.
Full trading day. Expected: buy at 09:30:13 / $107.89, sell at 11:39:22 / $129.43, profit $21.54 per share.
Morning trending phase. Expected: buy at 09:30:13 / $107.89, sell at 10:59:48 / $122.82, profit $14.93 per share.
Midday flat phase. Expected: buy at 12:11:04 / $115.29, sell at 12:20:10 / $128.89, profit $13.60 per share. Demonstrates the algorithm finds non-trivial trades even in low-volatility windows.

Late-day sell-off phase. Expected: buy at 14:30:32 / $117.19, sell at 14:39:25 / $120.01, profit $2.82 per share. Smallest magnitude of the four — the dataset's downtrend gives few profitable opportunities here.
`from` equals `to`, so there is no valid trade window. Expected 400 with `{ statusCode: 400, error: "Bad Request", message: "from must be strictly less than to", code: "INVALID_RANGE" }`.
Window predates the dataset's coverage period entirely. Expected 400 with `code: "OUT_OF_BOUNDS"` and a message naming the window.
`from` includes sub-second precision (`.500Z`). The DTO rejects anything other than `yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ssZ` second-precision UTC. Expected 400 with `code: "INVALID_TIMESTAMP"`.
